title: gymnadenia gracilis var. keiskei
intro: Gymnadenia gracilis var. keiskei, also known as Keiske's Orchid, is a type of flowering plant native to East Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and Korea, belonging to the orchid family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Gymnadenia gracilis var. keiskei include its potential to alleviate various health issues, such as fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, due to its active compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in traditional medicine, typically in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
